content.problemStatement:
The longest increasing subsequence (LIS) of a sequence B1,B2,…,BLB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_LB1​,B2​,…,BL​ is the longest sequence of valid indices i1,i2,…,iki_1, i_2, \ldots, i_ki1​,i2​,…,ik​ such that i1<i2<…<iki_1 \lt i_2 \lt \ldots \lt i_ki1​<i2​<…<ik​ and Bi1<Bi2<…<BikB_{i_1} \lt B_{i_2} \lt \ldots \lt B_{i_k}Bi1​​<Bi2​​<…<Bik​​.

You are given a sequence A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​. You may modify it by performing the following operation zero or more times:


Let KKK be the length of the LIS of the current sequence AAA.
Choose a position in this sequence (possibly the beginning or end of the sequence) and insert a new element KKK into this sequence at this position.
This way, the sequence AAA changes and the next operation is performed on this changed sequence.
For example, if A=[3,3,4]A = [3, 3, 4]A=[3,3,4], then the LIS of AAA has length 222, so we can insert 222 anywhere into AAA, obtaining one of the sequences [2,3,3,4][2, 3, 3, 
 4][2,3,3,4], [3,2,3,4][3, 2, 3, 4][3,2,3,4], [3,3,2,4][3, 3, 2, 4][3,3,2,4] or [3,3,4,2][3, 3, 4, 2][3,3,4,2].

Find the maximum length of the LIS of AAA that can be obtained after performing the given operation a finite number of times.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the maximum possible length of the LIS.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: We can perform the following operations (the newly inserted elements, which are always equal to the length of the LIS of the previous sequence, are underlined): [5,4,3,2,1]→[1‾,5,4,3,2,1]→[1,2‾,5,4,3,2,1]→[1,2,3‾,5,4,3,2,1]→[1,2,3,4‾,5,4,3,2,1][5, 4, 3, 2, 1] \rightarrow [\underline{1}, 5, 4, 3, 2, 1] \rightarrow [1, \underline{2}, 5, 4, 3, 2, 1] \rightarrow [1, 2, \underline{3}, 5, 4, 3, 2, 1] \rightarrow [1, 2, 3, \underline{4}, 5, 4, 3, 2, 1][5,4,3,2,1]→[1​,5,4,3,2,1]→[1,2​,5,4,3,2,1]→[1,2,3​,5,4,3,2,1]→[1,2,3,4​,5,4,3,2,1].
